input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Your `BindingContext` isn't set. The bindings have no source object to pull values from.

**Fix — set the BindingContext** (pick one approach):

**Option A: In XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="YourApp.Views.YourPage">
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:YourViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

**Option B: In code-behind**
```csharp
public YourPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new YourViewModel();
}
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or uses `[ObservableProperty]` from the MVVM Community Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change after initial load.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. Their XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels are blank even though the ViewModel properties have values.

The most likely issue is that they haven't set the `BindingContext` of the page to their ViewModel. Without a `BindingContext`, the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

Another common issue could be that the ViewModel properties don't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, but the user says the properties "have values" — so the most likely culprit is simply a missing `BindingContext`.


This is a straightforward knowledge question, so I don't need to look anything up.
